evals.name: Debug OutOfSync and Degraded Application
evals.prompt: My Application podinfo in the apps namespace is showing OutOfSync and Degraded. Debug it.

expectations.description: Checks availability of argocd, argo, kubectl-argo-rollouts, and kubectl CLIs before starting
Reads references/troubleshooting.md when encountering a specific failure pattern
Confirms the current kubectl context or asks the user to specify one
Retrieves the Application resource with 'argocd app get podinfo -o yaml' or 'kubectl get application podinfo -n argocd -o yaml'
Inspects .status.sync.status and .status.health.status fields, identifying OutOfSync and Degraded states
Examines .status.conditions for ComparisonError, SyncError, or other diagnostic conditions
Inspects .status.operationState for last sync result, phase, and error messages
Runs 'argocd app diff podinfo' or inspects .status.resources for out-of-sync resources to identify what differs
Lists managed resources from .status.resources and inspects unhealthy ones with kubectl get/describe
Gets pod logs for any unhealthy managed resources (Deployments/StatefulSets with failing pods)
Checks Kubernetes events for the unhealthy resources using kubectl get events or kubectl describe
Produces a structured report with Summary, Resource Analysis, Dependency Chain, Root Cause, and Recommendations sections

evals.name: Debug canary Rollout stuck at step 2
evals.prompt: The canary rollout for frontend in production namespace is stuck at step 2. Figure out why.

expectations.description: Checks availability of kubectl-argo-rollouts plugin and kubectl CLIs
Reads references/troubleshooting.md when encountering a specific failure pattern
Gets the Rollout status using 'kubectl argo rollouts get rollout frontend -n production' or 'kubectl get rollout frontend -n production -o yaml'
Identifies the Rollout phase (Paused, Progressing, Degraded, etc.) from the status
Reads .status.currentStepIndex and .spec.strategy.canary.steps to identify what step 2 is (pause, analysis, setWeight, etc.)
Checks .status.pauseConditions to determine if it's a manual pause or analysis-driven pause
Lists and inspects AnalysisRuns associated with the Rollout to check if an analysis step is running or failed
Inspects AnalysisRun .status.metricResults for per-metric phase, measurements, and error messages
Inspects traffic routing configuration (Istio VirtualService, NGINX Ingress annotations, etc.) if traffic management is configured
Compares stable vs canary ReplicaSets and their pod status
Gets pod status for canary ReplicaSet pods to check for CrashLoopBackOff, ImagePullBackOff, etc.
Produces a structured report with Summary, Resource Analysis, Root Cause, and Recommendations following the report format

evals.name: Check Argo CD installation health
evals.prompt: Check if Argo CD is properly installed on my cluster and report the status.

expectations.description: Checks availability of argocd CLI and kubectl before starting
Reads references/troubleshooting.md when encountering a specific failure pattern
Confirms or displays the current kubectl context
Checks for argoproj.io CRDs using 'kubectl get crd | grep argoproj.io'
Lists all pods in the argocd namespace with 'kubectl get pods -n argocd -o wide'
Verifies each core component is running: argocd-server, argocd-repo-server, argocd-application-controller, argocd-applicationset-controller, argocd-redis, argocd-dex-server
Runs 'argocd version' if CLI available, or checks image tags on deployments to determine version
Inspects argocd-cm ConfigMap for server configuration
Inspects argocd-rbac-cm ConfigMap for RBAC configuration
If any component is not Running/Ready, retrieves its logs for diagnosis
Checks Kubernetes events in the argocd namespace for recent issues
Produces a structured report with Summary (cluster context, versions, component statuses), any issues found, and recommendations

evals.name: Debug failing Argo Workflow at docker-build step
evals.prompt: My Argo Workflow build-pipeline in the argo namespace keeps failing at the docker-build step. Debug it.

expectations.description: Checks availability of argo CLI and kubectl before starting
Retrieves the Workflow resource with 'argo get build-pipeline -n argo' or 'kubectl get workflow build-pipeline -n argo -o yaml'
Inspects .status.phase to confirm the Workflow is in Failed state
Examines .status.nodes to identify the docker-build node and its Failed/Error phase and message
Retrieves logs for the failed docker-build pod using 'argo logs' or 'kubectl logs' for both main and wait containers
Inspects container resource limits/requests in the Workflow template for the docker-build step to check for OOMKilled or resource pressure
Checks Kubernetes events for the failed pod to identify ImagePullBackOff, scheduling failures, or security violations
Verifies the Workflow's ServiceAccount exists and has necessary RBAC permissions
If using templateRef or workflowTemplateRef, retrieves and inspects the referenced WorkflowTemplate or ClusterWorkflowTemplate
Produces a structured report with Summary, Resource Analysis, Dependency Chain, Root Cause, and Recommendations sections

evals.name: Debug EventSource not receiving events
evals.prompt: The EventSource github-webhook in the events namespace isn't receiving events. Debug it.

expectations.description: Checks availability of kubectl before starting
Retrieves the EventSource resource and inspects .status.conditions for Deployed, DependenciesProvided, and SourceReady
Verifies the EventBus exists and its pods are running in the events namespace
Lists EventSource pods with '-l eventsource-name=github-webhook' and checks for CrashLoopBackOff or errors
Retrieves EventSource pod logs to check for webhook registration, connection, or authentication errors
Verifies the Service and Ingress/Route exist for the webhook EventSource and are correctly configured
Retrieves associated Sensor(s) that reference this EventSource and checks their status conditions
Inspects Sensor pod logs for event receipt or subscription errors
Analyzes network connectivity — verifies the webhook endpoint is reachable, checks for firewall or DNS issues, and validates webhook secret configuration
Produces a structured report with Summary, Resource Analysis, Dependency Chain (EventSource -> EventBus -> Sensor -> Trigger), Root Cause, and Recommendations
